layman_headline: The crypto market is fearful and altcoins are bleeding — no clear setup worth risking capital on right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with Bitcoin dominance at 56%, meaning money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin for safety. Every swing candidate on our watchlist either has a bearish chart structure, is already overbought after a massive run (DEXE up 97% in a week), or lacks the technical confirmation needed for a high-quality entry. The best approach right now is patience — waiting for fear to peak and a genuine reversal signal to appear before committing capital.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch the market. No altcoin setup currently offers a good risk-to-reward ratio. Wait for either a clear oversold bounce with volume confirmation or a news catalyst that shifts sentiment.
expert_reasoning: Systematic review of all 10 candidates reveals no compelling swing setup. DEXE is the strongest mover (+97% 7d) but RSI at 77 screams overbought with imminent correction risk — this is a chase, not an entry. ZRO has the best RSI signal (31, near oversold) but chart bias is bearish (-0.70) with price below all MAs and no volume confirmation of a reversal. MON shows a bullish marubozu candle and approaching-oversold RSI, but the overall downtrend structure and sub-1.0 risk/reward ratio (0.46x) fail our quality threshold. GRASS had the biggest 7-day drop (-22%) for a mean-reversion play but printed a bearish engulfing candle with a -0.90 chart bias — selling is accelerating, not exhausting. With BTC dominance at 56.1% and Fear & Greed at 28, the environment strongly favors patience over forced entries in alts.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.1% actively suppresses altcoin performance — any BTC weakness could cause cascading alt selloffs
Fear & Greed at 28 is improving but hasn't reached capitulation levels (sub-15) that historically mark the best contrarian entries
BIP-110 fork uncertainty and Iran strike headlines create macro overhang that could push markets lower before recovering
watch_list: ZRO RSI dropping below 25 with a bullish reversal candle (hammer or morning star) — would signal genuine capitulation entry
Bitcoin ETF flow momentum — if inflows sustain above $200M/day for multiple sessions, alt sentiment typically improves within 5-7 days
Fear & Greed index trajectory — a move above 35 with BTC holding $63K would signal the fear phase is ending and alts may rotate
market_context: Crypto is in a 'Fear' regime (28/100, improving from 24.6 weekly avg) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.1%. Bitcoin ETFs just snapped an 8-week outflow streak with $197M in inflows, but geopolitical tensions (Iran strikes) and protocol-level debates (BIP-110) create uncertainty that keeps risk appetite suppressed for altcoins.
context_banner: Market fear is easing but hasn't capitulated — disciplined traders wait for the setup, not the urge to trade.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) improving from 24.6 weekly avg — sentiment recovering but not yet at levels that historically produce strong contrarian entries.
Best RSI reading is ZRO at 31 (near oversold) and MON at 41, but neither is confirmed by bullish chart structure or volume — insufficient for a high-confidence entry.
Bitcoin ETFs drew $197M snapping an 8-week outflow streak — first sign of institutional re-engagement, but effect hasn't flowed to alts yet.
BTC nearing Fidelity's power law support line tracked since 2015 — suggests BTC may find a floor here, but doesn't directly catalyze alt trades.
No candidate achieved a chart bias score above +0.60, and the best risk/reward ratios either came with bearish structures (PENGU 20x R/R but -0.70 bias) or lacked volume confirmation.
